markdown:
## **Overview**


1. After Napoleon’s defeat in 1815, **Louis XVIII** became king, restoring the Bourbon monarchy but keeping some revolutionary changes like the Charter of 1814.
2. His brother, **Charles X**, was more conservative, tried to bring back noble and church power, and was overthrown in the **July Revolution of 1830**.
3. The new king, **Louis Philippe**, was called the “Citizen King” because he seemed more liberal at first, but over time he mainly supported the wealthy middle class.
4. Discontent grew under Louis Philippe, especially among workers and students, leading to the **Revolution of 1848**.
5. The **Second Republic** (1848–1852) was created, introducing universal male suffrage, but it faced political conflict between moderates and radicals.
6. **Louis-Napoleon Bonaparte** (Napoleon’s nephew) was elected president of the Second Republic in 1848, showing the popularity of the Bonaparte name.
7. In 1852, Louis-Napoleon made himself **Emperor Napoleon III**, beginning the **Second Empire**, which mixed modernization with authoritarian rule.
8. His foreign policy had successes (Crimean War, early economic growth) but also disasters like the **Franco-Prussian War (1870–71)**, which ended his rule.

![The defeat of Napoleon lll at the hands of Bismarck.](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/a4ba7de2ac182ba7db65993f2c080311fb770128-4616x2993.png)

1. After the war, the **Third Republic** was created, and though it was unstable with many political parties, it lasted much longer than previous regimes.
2. By the late 19th century, the Third Republic faced crises like the **Dreyfus Affair**, but it also strengthened democracy and prepared France for World War I in 1914.
